summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Red Team CTF module (iteration 5) - X=pi by Carbi. All 23 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend UI elements verified working including exercise list, play mode, step execution, and scoring.
critical_code_review_comments: All CTF endpoints are SIMULATED/MOCKED with in-memory predefined exercise data - no real attacks executed
4 exercises covering different attack scenarios: Web (BEGINNER), Mobile (INTERMEDIATE), AD (ADVANCED), iOS Zero-Day (EXPERT)
Step execution returns simulated terminal outputs appropriate to each kill chain phase
Scoring and grading system works correctly (S+ grade for perfect completion)
Leaderboard contains 7 predefined operators with correct ranking
Frontend properly integrates with all backend CTF APIs via axios calls
Phase progression follows MITRE ATT&CK framework: RECONNAISSANCE -> INITIAL ACCESS -> PRIVILEGE ESCALATION -> PERSISTENCE -> LATERAL MOVEMENT -> EXFILTRATION
seed_data_creation: None - uses in-memory simulated exercise data defined in red_team_ctf.py
context_for_next_testing_agent: Red Team CTF module fully tested. All 5 CTF API endpoints verified. Frontend UI tested for exercise list view, play mode with step execution, terminal output display, scoring, and step navigation. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_ctf.py covers all endpoints with 23 test cases.

test_classes: TestCTFExercises (4 tests) - Exercise listing with 4 exercises and difficulty levels
TestCTFExerciseDetail (5 tests) - Exercise detail for EX-001 to EX-004 plus 404 handling
TestCTFStart (3 tests) - Starting exercises with default/custom operator names
TestCTFExecute (5 tests) - Step execution with output, points, mission completion
TestCTFLeaderboard (4 tests) - Leaderboard with 7 operators ranked by score
TestCTFFullFlow (2 tests) - Complete exercise flow and phase progression

ctf_module: GET /api/ctf/exercises - PASS (returns 4 exercises with total 1550 points)
GET /api/ctf/exercise/EX-001 - PASS (Operation Shadow Entry, 6 steps, BEGINNER)
GET /api/ctf/exercise/EX-002 - PASS (Operation Mobile Ghost, 6 steps, INTERMEDIATE)
GET /api/ctf/exercise/EX-003 - PASS (Operation Domain Domination, 5 steps, ADVANCED)
GET /api/ctf/exercise/EX-004 - PASS (Operation iOS Zero-Day, 5 steps, EXPERT)
POST /api/ctf/start {exercise_id: EX-002, operator_name: TestOp} - PASS (custom operator)
POST /api/ctf/execute {exercise_id: EX-001, step_index: 0} - PASS (returns output, +10 pts)
POST /api/ctf/execute {exercise_id: EX-001, step_index: 5} - PASS (mission_complete, grade S+)

ctf_module_ui: Home screen RED TEAM CTF card - VISIBLE (with flag icon)
CTF Exercise list view - 4 exercises displayed
Difficulty badges - BEGINNER (green), INTERMEDIATE (yellow), ADVANCED (orange), EXPERT (red)
Exercise cards - Name, description, objectives, steps count, points
Trophy icon for leaderboard toggle - VISIBLE
Exercise play view - Target network, score display, progress bar
Step display - Phase badge (color coded), title, description, command hint
EJECUTAR PASO button - Starts step execution
Step result - Terminal output (green monospace), points earned, Intel gained
SIGUIENTE PASO button - Advances to next step
Phase progression - RECONNAISSANCE -> INITIAL ACCESS -> PRIVILEGE ESCALATION verified

mocked_api_note: ALL CTF endpoints use SIMULATED/MOCKED in-memory exercise data. No real penetration testing or attacks are performed. This is for educational/training purposes only.
